title: Timing Metric Vocabulary
description: Canonical contract for timing/latency metric names submitted in MetricResults. Canonical keys are explicit-millisecond; legacy second-based aliases have been removed after the compatibility window. Advisory vocabulary: it names and reconciles timing keys but does NOT constrain MetricResults (which intentionally accepts arbitrary evaluator/user metrics).

x-naming-convention.description: Timing metric keys MUST carry an explicit unit suffix. `_ms` = milliseconds (canonical). Legacy `_s` suffix and unsuffixed keys are no longer accepted.

CanonicalTimingMetricName.description: Explicit-millisecond canonical timing keys. `response_time_ms` = MODEL CALL latency (per-config single) and `avg_response_time_ms` = its average are PRIMARY. `execution_time_ms` = total WALL-CLOCK execution of the evaluated call (user fn + scoring + overhead) — distinct from model latency; this is the canonical home for SDK function/wall-time durations. `latency_ms`/`avg_latency_ms`/`total_latency_ms` are accepted explicit-ms variants.

TimingMetricName.description: Any recognized timing key (canonical only; legacy _s aliases removed after compatibility window).

x-non-canonical-pending-migration.description: Emitted today but NOT canonical. Tracked for SDK migration in Traigent/Traigent#1225; consumers MAY map them during the compat window but new code MUST NOT introduce them. Semantics confirmed by the SDK agent (#1225).

function_duration.note: per-example wall-clock; folds into execution_time_ms (decision in #118). NOT minted as a new function_duration_ms key.

model_response_time.note: lossy rename of an already-ms model latency; emit raw ms as response_time_ms instead.
